description.en: Uses an XPath expression that specifies the nodes to be processed. An asterisk(*) selects the entire node-set. If this attribute is not set, all child nodes of the current node are selected.

description.en: The<xsl:value-of>element evaluates an XPath expression, converts it to a string, and writes that string to the result tree.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:value-of>element evaluates an XPath expression, converts it to a string, and writes that string to the result tree.

description.en: The<xsl:variable>element declares a global or local variable in a stylesheet and gives it a value. Because XSLT permits no side-effects, once the value of the variable has been established, it remains the same until the variable goes out of scope

info.excerpt: The<xsl:variable>element declares a global or local variable in a stylesheet and gives it a value. Because XSLT permits no side-effects, once the value of the variable has been established, it remains...

description.en: The<xsl:when>element always appears within an<xsl:choose>element, acting like a case statement.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:when>element always appears within an<xsl:choose>element, acting like a case statement.

description.en: The<xsl:with-param>element sets the value of a parameter to be passed into a template.

info.excerpt: The<xsl:with-param>element sets the value of a parameter to be passed into a template.
